source_paper_title: Positivstellensätze and Moment Problems with Universal Quantifiers
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Hu_2025, title={Positivstellensätze and Moment Problems with Universal Quantifiers}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2024.0402}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2024.0402}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Hu, Xiaomeng and Klep, Igor and Nie, Jiawang}, year={2025}, month=may }
context:
Section 7 (Conclusions and Discussions), page 26.

Our work leads to many intriguing questions to explore in the future. For instance, without assuming archimedeanness, is there a preordering version of Theorem 3.4? Equivalently, does there exist a clean algebraic reformulation of the compactness (or emptiness) of the set \(K\) given with a universal quantifier as in (1.1)? Is there an analog of the Krivine-Stengle Positivstellensatz for such sets \(K\)?
title: Krivine-Stengle Positivstellensatz analog for universal-quantifier-defined polynomial positivity sets
background_latex:
Let \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\) and \(y=(y_1,\dots,y_m)\) be real variables. Let \(Q\subseteq\mathbb{R}^m\) be a closed set, and let \(g=(g_1,\dots,g_s)\) be polynomials \(g_j\in\mathbb{R}[x,y]\). Define the feasible set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: g_1(x,y)\ge 0,\dots,g_s(x,y)\ge 0\ \forall y\in Q\}.
\]
Fix a finite nonnegative Borel measure \(\nu\) on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\) that satisfies the multivariate Carleman condition
\[
\sum_{d=0}^\infty \Big(\int y_j^{2d}\,d\nu(y)\Big)^{-\frac{1}{2d}}=\infty,\qquad j=1,\dots,m.
\]
For \(g_0:=1\), define the associated quadratic module (a convex cone in \(\mathbb{R}[x]\))
\[
\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]:=\left\{\sum_{j=0}^s \int \tau_j(x,y)\, g_j(x,y)\, d\nu(y):\ \tau_j\in\Sigma^2[x,y]\right\},
\]
where \(\Sigma^2[x,y]\) denotes the cone of sum-of-squares polynomials in \((x,y)\).
A classical (quantifier-free) Krivine--Stengle Positivstellensatz gives algebraic certificates for polynomial inequalities on semialgebraic sets, including non-compact and empty-set certificates, typically using preorderings and denominators.
In the present setting, the constraint description uses a universal quantifier \(\forall y\in Q\), and \(Q\) may be non-semialgebraic, so \(K\) may be non-semialgebraic as well.
problem_definition_latex:
Develop an analogue, for sets \(K\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) of the form
\[
K=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: g_1(x,y)\ge 0,\dots,g_s(x,y)\ge 0\ \forall y\in Q\},
\]
of the Krivine--Stengle Positivstellensatz: namely, give a certificate (in terms of algebraic data derived from \(g\), the universal quantifier over \(Q\), and possibly an auxiliary measure \(\nu\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\)) characterizing when a polynomial \(f\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) is nonnegative on \(K\), and in particular provide a corresponding algebraic infeasibility certificate characterizing when \(K=\emptyset\), without assuming an archimedean (compactness) condition on the underlying positivity cone.
latex:
Let \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\) and \(y=(y_1,\dots,y_m)\) be real variables. Let \(Q\subseteq\mathbb{R}^m\) be a closed set, and let \(g=(g_1,\dots,g_s)\) be polynomials \(g_j\in\mathbb{R}[x,y]\). Define the feasible set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: g_1(x,y)\ge 0,\dots,g_s(x,y)\ge 0\ \forall y\in Q\}.
\]
Fix a finite nonnegative Borel measure \(\nu\) on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\) that satisfies the multivariate Carleman condition
\[
\sum_{d=0}^\infty \Big(\int y_j^{2d}\,d\nu(y)\Big)^{-\frac{1}{2d}}=\infty,\qquad j=1,\dots,m.
\]
For \(g_0:=1\), define the associated quadratic module (a convex cone in \(\mathbb{R}[x]\))
\[
\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]:=\left\{\sum_{j=0}^s \int \tau_j(x,y)\, g_j(x,y)\, d\nu(y):\ \tau_j\in\Sigma^2[x,y]\right\},
\]
where \(\Sigma^2[x,y]\) denotes the cone of sum-of-squares polynomials in \((x,y)\).
A classical (quantifier-free) Krivine--Stengle Positivstellensatz gives algebraic certificates for polynomial inequalities on semialgebraic sets, including non-compact and empty-set certificates, typically using preorderings and denominators.
In the present setting, the constraint description uses a universal quantifier \(\forall y\in Q\), and \(Q\) may be non-semialgebraic, so \(K\) may be non-semialgebraic as well.

Develop an analogue, for sets \(K\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) of the form
\[
K=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: g_1(x,y)\ge 0,\dots,g_s(x,y)\ge 0\ \forall y\in Q\},
\]
of the Krivine--Stengle Positivstellensatz: namely, give a certificate (in terms of algebraic data derived from \(g\), the universal quantifier over \(Q\), and possibly an auxiliary measure \(\nu\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\)) characterizing when a polynomial \(f\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) is nonnegative on \(K\), and in particular provide a corresponding algebraic infeasibility certificate characterizing when \(K=\emptyset\), without assuming an archimedean (compactness) condition on the underlying positivity cone.
open_problem_quote: Our work leads to many intriguing questions to explore in the future. For instance, without assuming archimedeanness, is there a preordering version of Theorem 3.4? Equivalently, does there exist a clean algebraic reformulation of the compactness (or emptiness) of the set \(K\) given with a universal quantifier as in (1.1)? Is there an analog of the Krivine-Stengle Positivstellensatz for such sets \(K\)?
counterexample_suitability_rationale: Potential candidate certificates can be tested computationally on randomly generated low-degree instances \(g\) and sets \(Q\) (especially when \(Q\) is finite or compact and sampling is feasible), but the conjectured certificate form is itself not specified, so there is no fixed finite search space for counterexamples.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary loop could explore candidate algebraic certificate templates (e.g., preordering-like combinations involving integrals/moments over \(Q\)) and score them by empirical soundness/completeness on many synthetic test instances where nonnegativity on \(K\) can be checked numerically (e.g., via discretization of \(Q\) or worst-case search). However, faithfulness to a universal theorem and the need for symbolic proof limit fully automated progress.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Hu–Klep–Nie, 2024) establishes a Putinar/Jacobi-type Positivstellensatz for universally quantified constraint sets \(K=\{x: g_j(x,y)\ge0\  orall y\in Q\}\) using the integral quadratic module \(\mathrm{QM}[g,
u]\) built from SOS multipliers \(	au_j(x,y)\) integrated against a Carleman-determinate measure \(
u\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(
u)=Q\). In the Archimedean case, strict positivity \(f>0\) on \(K\) implies \(f\in \mathrm{QM}[g,
u]\), and emptiness is characterized by \(-1\in \mathrm{QM}[g,
u]\). In the non-Archimedean case they obtain a perturbation result: \(f\ge0\) on \(K\) iff for all \(\varepsilon>0\) there exists \(r\) with \(f+\varepsilon\Omega_r\in \mathrm{QM}[g,
u]\), where \(\Omega_r\) is an explicit high-degree positive polynomial.

The open problem asks for a genuine Krivine–Stengle analogue in this quantified setting, i.e., a denominator/preordering-style certificate for nonnegativity and especially an algebraic infeasibility certificate for \(K=\emptyset\) without Archimedean assumptions. Among the forward citations provided, the closest partial progress is "Positivstellens" atze for polynomial matrices with universal quantifiers", which extends the \(\mathrm{QM}[\cdot,
u]\) methodology to polynomial matrix inequalities and proves several non-Archimedean positivity certificates with explicit multipliers/denominators (e.g., \(\|x\|^{2N}\), \((e^	op x)^N\), or \(	heta^{N_\varepsilon}\)) under additional structural hypotheses such as homogeneity or restriction to \(\mathbb R_+^n\). The remaining citing works are primarily algorithmic or complexity-oriented: they develop KKT-based or Moment–SOS methods for semi-infinite/robust problems with structured parameter sets (polyhedral or semialgebraic) or provide degree bounds for matrix Putinar-type certificates. None supplies a full Krivine–Stengle-type Positivstellensatz (with denominators and an emptiness certificate) for general universally quantified scalar constraints over possibly non-semialgebraic \(Q\).

Thus, based on the provided forward-citation set, the problem remains open. Promising directions suggested by partial results include: (i) importing homogenization/Putinar–Vasilescu and P'olya-type multiplier techniques to the integral quadratic module \(\mathrm{QM}[g,
u]\); (ii) identifying structural conditions on \(g\) and \(Q\) under which a denominator certificate \(p(x)f\in \mathrm{QM}[g,
u]\) (or a preordering analogue) holds; and (iii) developing a quantified analogue of Krivine–Stengle infeasibility certificates, potentially via moment-duality and determinacy (Carleman) combined with homogenization for unbounded \(K\).

sources.title: Positivstellens" atze for polynomial matrices with universal quantifiers
sources.source_url: (source URL not provided in forward-citation analysis)
sources.theorem_or_result:
They prove several Positivstellensätze for universally quantified polynomial matrix inequality sets. In particular, for 
\[X:=\{x\in\mathbb R^n: G(x,y)\succeq 0\ \forall y\in Y\},\]
with a Borel measure \(\nu\) supported on \(Y\) satisfying Carleman, they show:
- (Archimedean case) If \(Q_p(G,\nu)\) is Archimedean and \(F(x)\in S[x]_p\) is positive definite on \(X\), then
\[F(x)=S_0(x)+\int_Y \langle S(x,y),G(x,y)\rangle_p\,d\nu(y)\in Q_p(G,\nu),\]
for SOS matrices \(S_0\in S[x]_p\), \(S\in S[x,y]_{pq}\) (Theorem 3.1).
- (Non-Archimedean generalizations) Under homogeneity/orthant hypotheses they obtain denominator-type certificates such as
\[\|x\|^{2N}F(x)=S_0(x)+\int_Y\langle S(x,y),G(x,y)\rangle_p\,d\nu(y)\]
(Theorem 4.1) and a Pólya-type \((e^\top x)^N\) certificate on \(\mathbb R_+^n\cap X\) (Theorem 4.2), plus inhomogeneous \(\theta^{N_\varepsilon}(F+\varepsilon\theta^{d_F}I_p)\) variants (Corollary 4.1).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a Krivine–Stengle-type Positivstellensatz (including infeasibility/emptiness certificates) for sets \(K=\{x: g_j(x,y)\ge 0\ \forall y\in Q\}\) without Archimedean assumptions. This paper extends Hu–Klep–Nie’s Archimedean, measure-based quadratic-module certificate to the matrix/PMI setting and, importantly, provides some non-Archimedean Positivstellensätze with denominators/multipliers (\(\|x\|^{2N}\), \((e^\top x)^N\), or \(\theta^{N_\varepsilon}\)) under additional structural assumptions (homogeneity in \(x\), or restriction to \(\mathbb R_+^n\)). However, it does not establish a full Krivine–Stengle analogue for general (inhomogeneous) universally quantified scalar constraints, nor does it give a general algebraic emptiness certificate for \(K=\emptyset\) in this quantified, potentially non-semialgebraic setting. Thus it is partial progress: it supplies non-Archimedean positivity certificates in special regimes and develops techniques likely relevant to a future Krivine–Stengle-type theorem.

sources.title: A Global Approach for Generalized Semi-Infinite Programs with Polyhedral Parameter Sets: X. Hu, J. Nie, S. Zhong
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops a disjunctive KKT-based semidefinite (Moment–SOS) algorithm for polynomial generalized semi-infinite programs (GSIPs) with polyhedral parameter sets. A central structural result is the decomposition of the KKT parameter set for the inner minimization 
\[v(x)=\inf\{g(x,u): Au\ge b(x)\}\]
into finitely many semi-algebraic pieces indexed by rank-
\(r=\mathrm{rank}(A)\) subsets \(J\) of active constraints: for \(K(x)=\{u:\exists\lambda\ge0,\ \nabla_u g(x,u)-A^T\lambda=0,\ 0\le Au-b(x)\perp\lambda\ge0\}\),
\[K(x)=\bigcup_{J\in\mathcal P} K_J(x),\]
where each \(K_J(x)\) admits an explicit description using the partial Lagrange multiplier expression
\(\lambda_J(x,u)=(A_JA_J^T)^{-1}A_J\nabla_u g(x,u)\) and complementarity constraints (Theorem 3.4 and Proposition 3.3).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a Krivine–Stengle-type Positivstellensatz (including emptiness certificates) for sets \(K=\{x: g_j(x,y)\ge0\ \forall y\in Q\}\) without archimedeanity, potentially with non-semi-algebraic \(Q\), using measure-based quadratic modules \(\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]\). This citing paper does not provide such a Positivstellensatz or an algebraic infeasibility certificate for universally quantified constraints in that generality. Instead, it treats a different (more structured) universal quantifier setting arising from GSIPs where the quantified set is polyhedral (and may depend on \(x\)): \(U(x)=\{u: Au\ge b(x)\}\). The paper’s contribution is algorithmic: it replaces the universal constraint by solving/relaxing an inner polynomial optimization over a polyhedron via KKT conditions, then solves resulting polynomial programs by Moment–SOS. The KKT-set decomposition and partial Lagrange multiplier expressions are potentially useful tools for handling universal quantifiers over polyhedra, but they do not yield a Krivine–Stengle-style representation of \(\{f\ge0\text{ on }K\}\) nor a measure-based certificate for \(K=\emptyset\) in the non-archimedean, non-semi-algebraic \(Q\) setting of the open problem.

sources.title: Moment-SOS Relaxation Methods for Generalized Semi-Infinite Programs
sources.source_url: (source URL not provided in forward-citation analysis)
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper develops Algorithm 3.3: a hierarchy of polynomial optimization relaxations for polynomial generalized semi-infinite programs (GSIPs) of the form \(\min f(x)\) s.t. \(g(x,u)\ge 0\ \forall u\in U(x)\), using “polynomial extensions” \(q(x)\) with \(q(\hat x)=\hat u\) and \(q(x)\in U(x)\ \forall x\in X\) to generate finitely many polynomial constraints \(g(x,q(x))\ge 0\). It proves (i) finite convergence: if at some loop \(\hat x_k\) satisfies \(g(\hat x_k,u)\ge 0\ \forall u\in U(\hat x_k)\), then \(\hat x_k\) is globally optimal (Theorem 3.4); and (ii) asymptotic convergence: under continuity of the value function \(v(x)=\min_j\min_{u\in U(x)} g_j(x,u)\) at an accumulation point and equicontinuity of the extensions, any accumulation point \(x^*\) of \(\{\hat x_k\}\) satisfies \(v(x^*)=0\) and is a global minimizer (Theorem 3.5). It also shows that when the inner minimization in \(u\) is convex and KKT-attained, the universal constraint can be reformulated via KKT conditions into a single (polynomial/rational) optimization problem (Theorems 5.1–5.2).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for a Krivine–Stengle-type Positivstellensatz (including emptiness/infeasibility certificates) for sets \(K=\{x: g_j(x,y)\ge 0\  orall y\in Q\}\) possibly non-semi-algebraic, using an auxiliary measure \(
u\) and the cone \(\mathrm{QM}[g,
u]=\{\sum_j\int 	au_j(x,y)g_j(x,y)\,d
u(y):	au_j\in\Sigma^2[x,y]\}\), without archimedean assumptions. Hu–Nie do not address measure-based quadratic modules, Carleman conditions, or representation theorems for \(f\ge 0\) on \(K\). Instead they propose algorithmic Moment-SOS hierarchies for solving polynomial GSIPs/SIPs by iteratively adding finitely many polynomial constraints derived from polynomial selections \(q_k(x)\in U(x)\). This provides computational machinery for optimization/feasibility of universally quantified polynomial constraints in semialgebraic \(U(x)\), and can detect infeasibility of the relaxation problems, but it is not a Positivstellensatz characterizing \(f\ge 0\) on \(K\) nor an algebraic emptiness certificate for \(K=\emptyset\) in the non-archimedean, possibly non-semi-algebraic \(Q\) setting.

sources.title: Lagrange multiplier expressions for matrix polynomial optimization and tight relaxations
sources.source_url: (source URL not provided in forward-citation analysis)
sources.theorem_or_result: Main technical results: (a) Under a global nondegeneracy condition for a matrix polynomial constraint (equivalently, a certain polynomial matrix P(x) has full column rank for all x∈C^n), every KKT pair (x,Λ) for matrix polynomial optimization \(\min f(x)\) s.t. \(G(x)\succeq 0\) satisfies \(\Lambda=\Theta(x)\) for an explicit symmetric matrix polynomial \(\Theta(x)\) (a “Lagrange multiplier matrix expression”). (b) Using this, they define a strengthened moment/SOS hierarchy for the KKT reformulation and prove finite convergence (tightness) under assumptions including: f_min is attained at a KKT point, the multiplier expression exists, and \(\mathrm{Ideal}[G\Theta,\nabla f-\nabla G^*[\Theta]]+\mathrm{QM}[G,\Theta]\) is Archimedean (Theorem 4.2).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a Krivine–Stengle-type Positivstellensatz (including infeasibility/emptiness certificates) for sets defined by universal quantifiers \(g_j(x,y)\ge 0\ \forall y\in Q\), potentially non-semi-algebraic and non-Archimedean, using data such as \(g\) and a measure \(\nu\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\). The citing paper is about matrix polynomial optimization with semidefinite constraints \(G(x)\succeq 0\) and strengthened moment/SOS relaxations via KKT conditions and polynomial multiplier expressions. It does not develop a Krivine–Stengle analogue for universally quantified constraints, nor does it address non-Archimedean Positivstellensatz/infeasibility certificates for such \(K\). It only briefly notes that \(G(x)\succeq 0\) can be written as a semi-infinite constraint \(y^T G(x) y\ge 0\ \forall \|y\|\le 1\) and compares numerically to existing SIP moment-SOS methods, but provides no new Positivstellensatz for the universal-quantifier setting.

sources.title: On the complexity of matrix Putinar's Positivstellensätz
sources.theorem_or_result:
Main results are effective (degree/complexity) bounds for matrix Putinar-type Positivstellensätze on semialgebraic sets defined by polynomial matrix inequalities. In particular, if the quadratic module 
\(\mathrm{QM}[G]_\ell\) is Archimedean (e.g. \((1-\|x\|_2^2)I_\ell\in \mathrm{QM}[G]_\ell\)) and \(F(x)\succeq F_{\min}I_\ell\succ 0\) on \(K=\{x: G(x)\succeq 0\}\), then for an explicit polynomially-bounded degree \(k\) (Theorem 1.1 / 5.1) one has a certificate
\[F = S + \sum_{i=1}^t P_i(x)^T G(x) P_i(x),\]
with \(S\in \Sigma[x]_\ell\) and \(\deg(S),\deg(P_i^TGP_i)\le k\). For unbounded \(K\), a Putinar–Vasilescu-type certificate is proved: if additionally the leading homogeneous part \(F_{\mathrm{hom}}\succ 0\) on \(\mathbb R^n\), then for explicit \(k\), \((1+\|x\|_2^2)^k F\in \mathrm{QM}[G]_\ell\) with controlled degree (Theorem 1.2 / 5.3).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a Krivine–Stengle-type Positivstellensatz (including infeasibility/emptiness certificates, non-Archimedean/noncompact setting) for sets defined by universal quantifiers \(g_j(x,y)\ge 0\ \forall y\in Q\), potentially non-semialgebraic, using an integral/SOS cone \(\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]\). Huang’s paper does not address this quantifier/integration setting, nor Krivine–Stengle-style certificates with denominators or emptiness certificates for such \(K\). It instead gives effective degree bounds for (matrix) Putinar and Putinar–Vasilescu certificates on semialgebraic sets \(\{x: G(x)\succeq 0\}\), and briefly notes that a matrix inequality can be rewritten as a universal quantifier \(y^T G(x) y\ge 0\ \forall \|y\|=1\), suggesting future work on universal-quantifier Positivstellensätze. Thus it is only tangentially relevant as a complexity/technique reference, not a solution to the Krivine–Stengle analogue sought.
